codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature entry, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, and commit diff for ec28e9e. The code changes are appropriately scoped to the regressions the audit found: `src/app/globals.css` restores the BETTER dark shadcn token/radius contract, and `src/components/ui/__tests__/card-theme-contract.test.ts` adds focused regression coverage for popover tokens and the shared 8px radius. The transcript/handoff also show a complete browser walk across all 13 atlas surfaces plus deferred-chunk checks for the four new surfaces, which credibly satisfies VAL-POLISH-001 and VAL-POLISH-002. I did not find any blocking correctness gaps in the implementation reviewed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared design guidance is precise about card glass tokens and the 8px radius, but it does not explicitly extend that contract to shadcn popover/dialog/sheet surfaces. This review feature had to repair and lock those overlay tokens during final polish, so documenting that overlays should stay on the same #101010 / white BETTER canvas contract would reduce future regressions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/design-system.md:27-29 and 142-175 plus mission AGENTS.md:138-141 document card surface/border/radius expectations, but neither mentions popover/overlay tokens. The reviewed feature then added explicit popover assertions in src/components/ui/__tests__/card-theme-contract.test.ts and restored the popover values in src/app/globals.css (commit ec28e9e6ecaba9ae05f3120f48df5b898a5d2c2a).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker had to mark `followedProcedure=false` solely because baseline validation exposed an obviously in-scope visual regression that they then fixed with a focused failing test and full revalidation. That suggests the baseline-validation procedure should explicitly allow repairing clear in-scope polish regressions before escalating.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The handoff for 2026-03-24T05-24-33-885Z__polish-visual-audit-and-regressions__8e4d6100-899b-4ecb-a8c8-2e4f7a6ee478.json at lines 73-79 records the only deviation as '1.3 Baseline Validation'. The transcript skeleton shows the subsequent sequence was a focused failing contract test, a scoped globals.css fix, a passing focused rerun, then full lint/typecheck/test/build plus browser audit.

summary: Pass. The reviewed feature appropriately fixed the audit findings it uncovered, added regression coverage for the restored shadcn theme contract, and supplied credible browser and chunk-loading evidence that the 9 existing surfaces still render correctly while the 4 new surfaces remain deferred under the <5 KB compressed target.
